title: Node Fetch, Redirect Control, Streams, Timeouts, and Bounded Bodies · Inference Brief: Body Cleanup
summary: Each request creates unrelated policy, follows redirects automatically, checks only Content-Length, buffers unlimited decoded bodies, and loses abort causes. Predict authorization, URL identity, runtime admission, Promise ownership, resource bounds, accessibility impact, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the crawler ts body cleanup increment in bounded Fetch response adapter. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed…
objectives: Cancel or drain bodies by explicit policy and prove sockets, readers, timers, and listeners settle on every exit.
Compose connection-adjacent, response, body, attempt, job, and shutdown deadlines while retaining the first abort reason.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

Competency probe: Cancel or drain bodies by explicit policy and prove sockets, readers, timers, and listeners settle on every exit.

For Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Defend the Body Cleanup model · Theory Body Cleanup, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Garbage collection promptly releases abandoned Response bodies.
